falsification: No executable state encoder/decoder or normalization rule exists.
rollback: Keep as DOC_ONLY and do not wire into runtime.

falsification: Measured code uses a different alpha or cannot reproduce phi.
rollback: Gate claim behind docs until deterministic test vector exists.

falsification: Table has fewer/more than 42 entries or period test fails.
rollback: Do not close known bug; keep doc-only until table/test fixed.

falsification: No signal artifact or correlation baseline is present.
rollback: Label as model; do not assert biological measurement.

falsification: No layer composition implementation exists.
rollback: Preserve as navigation semantics only.

falsification: Entropy formula differs from documented test vectors.
rollback: Keep claim out of runtime until vector set passes.

falsification: Checksum differs across ABI or is described as cryptographic when not.
rollback: Classify as integrity/checksum only.

falsification: Integrity script fails or required digest missing.
rollback: Block promotion and keep previous artifact hashes.

falsification: Q16 conversion or recurrence test exceeds tolerance.
rollback: Demote performance/physics claims to DOC_ONLY.

falsification: Buffer dimensions or bounds do not match capacity rule.
rollback: Fallback to explicit bounds documentation.

falsification: VOID is silently patched or attractor #22 loses flag.
rollback: Keep #22 flagged; no silent patch.

falsification: gcd(delta, dimension) != 1 in traversal config.
rollback: Reject traversal table and keep previous route.

falsification: Claim is presented as secure encryption without threat model.
rollback: State experimental only; require security review.

falsification: Metaphor is promoted to measured physical claim.
rollback: Demote to parable/model until instrumented evidence exists.

falsification: No phonetic/acoustic dataset or reproducible transform exists.
rollback: Keep as semantic metadata and avoid runtime claim.

falsification: No dataset split, leakage control, or metric artifact exists.
rollback: Document as schema; no financial recommendation.

falsification: Hot path allocates heap or JNI path hides allocation.
rollback: Move allocation to cold path or keep module out of hot path.

falsification: Branch-heavy path is claimed branchless without asm/C evidence.
rollback: Demote claim and add measured microbenchmark before restore.

falsification: No command or artifact demonstrates rollback/guard.
rollback: Keep as runbook requirement; do not call enterprise-ready.

falsification: Generated docs differ from checked-in docs.
rollback: Regenerate and recommit deterministic artifacts.
